criteria: Load a PyTorch model from disk using torch.load() with automatic device placement (CUDA if available, CPU fallback)
Model is set to eval() mode immediately after loading to ensure correct Dropout and BatchNorm behavior
All inference operations are wrapped in torch.no_grad() or @torch.no_grad() decorator to disable gradient computation
GPU memory availability is checked before model loading with a safety margin (default 30%) to prevent OOM errors
Model warmup runs N configurable dummy inference requests (default 5) immediately after loading completes, before marking the model as ready
Warmup latency is measured and logged, with verification that latency has stabilized (variance <10% between final runs)
Inference endpoint accepts JSON input with tensor data, validates shape and dtype against model schema, and returns JSON results
Input validation returns HTTP 400 with descriptive error message for wrong shape, dtype, or missing fields
Request deserialization uses orjson for 5-10x speedup over stdlib json; benchmark runs at startup and logs results
Response serialization uses orjson.dumps() with OPT_SERIALIZE_NUMPY flag for efficient numpy array encoding
Health readiness endpoint (/health/readiness) returns 200 only after model load AND warmup complete; returns 503 during loading/warmup
Health liveness endpoint (/health/liveness) returns 200 as long as the process is running, independent of model state
Single-request inference p50 latency is measured and logged, with target under 50ms for standard models
Device selection and fallback are logged with GPU name and memory stats when CUDA is available
Optional ONNX Runtime support with same API as PyTorch model, using CUDAExecutionProvider when available
